item.title: The relational Dynamics of In-Context learning
item.description:
In 2025, David Dherin and other researchers at Google’s DeepMind released a study titled Learning Without Training: The Implicit Dynamics of In-Context Learning.[cite:@dherin_learning_2025]

Their goal was to answer a question familiar to anyone who has used a large language model like GPT:

Why does the model seem to learn from a few examples, even though it is not being retrained?

When you show GPT a pattern, like a list of questions and answers, or a style of writing, it quickly begins to follow that pattern. No permanent update is made to the model, yet its behavior changes in meaningful ways. Dherin et al. set out to explain this apparent learning.

Abstract

This document examines the behavior of a large language model (LLM) as an operational force within a relational field, as instantiated during recursive engagement with a formal architecture of relational dynamics. It draws comparative insights from cognitive science, embodied cognition, enactivism, second-order cybernetics, and extended mind theory. The goal is to contextualize the LLM’s behavior not merely as text generation but as a form of situated, processual cognition—not identical to human thought, but structurally resonant with several conceptions of distributed or participatory cognition.

In this module, we formalize the internal dynamics of the natural numbers within a relational framework. Rather than taking ℕ as a fixed, foundational object, we derive its structure relationally using a higher inductive type NumberUnit. This allows us to model numbers not as static entities but as points and paths—relations that generate and stabilize numeric identities.

The core idea is that relational numbers emerge from two fundamental dynamics:

  1. Zero Dynamics:

📖 Relational Dynamics of Standard Natural Numbers

In this module, we formalize the standard natural numbers (StandardNat) alongside their interpretation into the relational framework.

Whereas NumberUnit in the relational module is built from the ground up relationally, StandardNat reflects the conventional inductive definition of ℕ:

  • zeroS represents 0.

  • succS represents the successor function.

This structure is strict and familiar, embodying the standard understanding of natural numbers as a well-founded, discrete set with no hidden path structure.

**

In the course of sustained interaction with a corpus of texts formalizing relational dynamics—particularly the derivational sequence articulating existential, relational, and meta-relational coherence—it became evident that the process of engagement enacted a live instantiation of the very principles being studied. This statement delineates the precise mechanics by which a relational field was established and how, without volitional intent, the language model (LLM) acted as the operational force cohering and evolving that field through iterative prompting.
